JOB SUMMARY:

The Navy and Marine Corps team offers innovative, exciting and meaningful work linking military and civilian talents to achieve our mission and safeguard our freedoms. Department of the Navy provides competitive salaries, comprehensive benefits, and extensive professional development and training. From pipefitters to accountants, scientists to engineers, doctors to nurses-the careers and opportunities to make a difference are endless. Civilian careers-where purpose and patriotism unite!

The position serves in the Marine Corps Installations Command (MCICOM), Facilities Systems Branch (FSB), located in the Marine Corps Information Technology Center (MCITC), Kansas City, MO., responsible for the technical development, implementation, and maintenance of Information Technology (IT) systems used within the Marine Corps.

TRAVEL REQUIRED

* Not Required

RELOCATION AUTHORIZED

* No

KEY REQUIREMENTS

* You must be a US Citizen.
* Must be registered for Selective Service, see Legal & Regulatory Guidance.
* Suitable for Federal employment as determined by background investigation.
* Selectee may be required to successfully complete a probationary period.
* You must obtain and maintain a secret security clearance.



DUTIES:

·         Analyze and define data requirements and specifications.

·         Design, normalize, develop, install, and implement databases.

·         Install, configure, and maintain database management systems software.

·         Monitor database performance, file organization and structure, file management and ensure compatible operating systems in accordance with specific database systems and database application requirements.



QUALIFICATIONS REQUIRED:

In order to qualify for this position, your resume must provide sufficient experience and/or education, knowledge, skills, and abilities, to perform the duties of the specific position for which you are being considered.  Your resume is the key means we have for evaluating your skills, knowledge, and abilities, as they relate to this position. Therefore, we encourage you to be clear and specific when describing your experience.

Your resume must demonstrate at least one year of specialized experience at or equivalent to the GS-09 grade level or pay band in the Federal service or equivalent experience in the private or public sector. Specialized experience is defined as experience that is typically in or related to the work of the position to be filled and has equipped you with the particular knowledge, skills, and abilities, to successfully perform the duties of the position. Specialized experience must demonstrate the following: Attention to Detail: Takes a thorough, conscientious approach to work performed; Customer Service: Identifies a client or customer need and provides a quality product or service. Oral Communication: Expresses information via oral presentation; AND Problem Solving: Identifies problem, ascertains root cause and impact, and provides recommendations for resolution.  Specialized experience for this position also involves performing database management using Oracle 10g or 11g; developing and maintaining databases utilizing Microsoft (MS) Structured Query Language (SQL) Server 2008 or 2012, Windows and Linux Operating Systems (OS), and high level programming language such as Procedural Language/Structured Query Language (PL/SQL); maintaining the integrity and security of databases; configuring database software; AND verifying/validating system updates.

OR

Possess a Ph.D. or equivalent doctoral degree OR 3 full years of progressively higher level graduate education leading to a Ph.D. or equivalent doctoral degree in computer science, engineering, information science, information systems management, mathematics, operations research, statistics, or technology management or degree that provided a minimum of 24 semester hours in one or more of the fields identified above and required the development or adaptation of applications, systems or networks.

Description:
Chancellor's Office Statement
Join our team at the California State University, Office of the Chancellor, and make a difference in providing access to higher education. We are currently seeking experienced candidates for the position of Administrative Support Coordinator. The CSU Chancellor's Office, located on the waterfront adjacent to the Aquarium of the Pacific in downtown Long Beach, is the headquarters for the nation's largest and most diverse system of higher education. The CSU Chancellor's Office offers a premium benefit package that includes outstanding vacation, health, and dental plans; a fee waiver education program; membership in the California Public Employees Retirement System (PERS); and 14 paid holidays a year.

Salary
The salary range for this classification is $3,115 to $5,161 per month.

Position Information
The California State University, Office of the Chancellor, is seeking an Administrative Support Coordinator performs a full range of clerical and administrative support using office technology and systems, while managing the Financial Services reception desk, including answering the department's main telephone lines, receiving and distributing mail, processing incoming checks, and handling general inquiries. The incumbent also provides administrative assistance to five Financial Services departments: Administration, Accounting, Management & Accounting Practices Office, Sponsored Programs Administration, and Systemwide Financial Standards & Reporting. This includes assistance with financial tracking, travel and related travel arrangements, equipment inventory, editing and proofreading of outgoing internal and external communications, supply purchasing and tracking, file maintenance and the development of procedures for administrative duties and actions within Financial Services. Additionally, they support the Assistant Director with website and SharePoint maintenance, including some HTML and WYSIWYG programing on select Financial Services internal and external websites, as well as complete ad hoc projects as assigned by the Assistant Director or Assistant Vice Chancellor. The Administrative Support Coordinator will have additional duties assisting Sponsored Programs and Accounts Payable, including more advanced administrative duties such as SharePoint upkeep and maintenance, processing financial transactions, and Travel/eTravel support. This will include the planning of training, meetings and events for various campus entities and Chancellor's Office staff, both at the CO and at campuses throughout the CSU system, as well as ad hoc special projects within Sponsored Program.

APPLICANTS CALLED IN TO INTERVIEW WILL BE GIVEN A SKILLS TEST.

Responsibilities
Under the general direction of the Assistant Director, Financial Services Administration, the Administrative Support Coordinator will be responsible for:

FINANCIAL SERVICES RECEPTION DESK

Client Resources Coordinator II  Youth Build / AYES

Archdiocesan Youth Employment Services (AYES) of Catholic Charities of Los Angeles is seeking a quality individual with strong commitment to excellence and community service. AYES is the leading youth provider of career and employment services in Los Angeles.

The YouthBuild program provide low-income young people the opportunity to learn construction skills through building affordable housing for homeless and low-income people in their neighborhoods and other community assets such as schools, playgrounds, and community centers. This program is for unemployed young people who left high school without a diploma. YouthBuild is an opportunity to reclaim their educations, gain the skills they need for employment, become leaders in their communities and break the cycle of poverty with a commitment to work, education, family, and community.

* Assist clients to complete and submit application forms for government and other social service agencies.
* Provide support services to individuals residing in the community.
* Make routine community contacts with such institutions as schools, medical facilities and social service department.
* Coordinate and assist in the preparation of client orientations.
* Assist in the scheduled mailing for screening interviews.
* May evaluate actual living conditions of clients through home visits.
* Prepare reports and related analysis for data.

Qualifications

* Two years college required.
* Six to twelve months of related experience.
* Ability to write clearly and informatively.
* Must have excellent communication and organizational skills.
